preview.summary: World Cup finalists France meet Iraq, who ended a 40-year absence from the tournament. Didier Deschamps' star-studded side are clear favorites, but the Lions of Mesopotamia arrive on a wave of belief after their dramatic playoff run.
preview.homeForm: Perennial contenders and 2022 finalists, among the tournament favorites in Deschamps' final campaign.
preview.awayForm: Qualified via the intercontinental playoffs, beating Bolivia 2-1; first World Cup appearance since Mexico 1986.
preview.homeKey: Kylian Mbappé, captain and France's all-time scoring threat.
preview.awayKey: Aymen Hussein, record scorer whose goals secured qualification.
storylines: Deschamps' farewell tournament after 13 years in charge
Mbappé and Ballon d'Or winner Dembélé lead a fearsome attack
Iraq's 40-year wait ends against the toughest possible opener
preview.h2h: France and Iraq have no significant competitive history, meeting here for the first time on the World Cup stage.
